title: ADMP Data Subject Rights (ADM Restrictions)
description: Three rights provided to data subjects where automated decision-making (and by extension profiling) produces legal effects or significantly affects the data subject. Collectively the 'ADM Restrictions'. Source: JPDP ADMP Guideline (PCP No. 3/2025 substantive content).

rights.name: Right to Refuse
rights.summary: Data subjects have the right to refuse to be subjected to a decision based solely on automated decision-making (including profiling) which produces legal effects concerning them or significantly affects them.

scope.basedSolelyOn: A decision-making process that is entirely automated such that human influence is excluded. A process may still be considered solely automated if a human merely inputs the data and the decision-making is then carried out by an automated system.

controllerObligations: Provide a clear mechanism for the data subject to exercise the Right to Refuse
Where refusal is exercised, provide an alternative non-automated path or stop the processing
Document the refusal and the alternative provided
rights.exceptionsNote: Right to Refuse can be excluded where Exceptions apply (contract necessity, legal compliance, explicit consent).

rights.name: Right to Information
rights.summary: A right to information on the automated decision-making being undertaken — the transparency requirement.
controllerObligations: Notify the data subject where processing involves automated decision-making
Provide meaningful information about the logic involved
Explain the importance and potential impact of the processing
meaningfulInformationLogic: The type of information collected or used in creating the profile or making the automated decision
What the likely impact will be / how it is likely to affect the data subject
rights.exceptionsNote: The Right to Information is RETAINED even where Exceptions apply. Transparency is mandatory regardless of Exception or Exemption status — so that any explicit consent (where relied on as an Exception) is informed.

rights.name: Right to Human Review
rights.summary: A right to request a human review of the automated decision-making.
controllerObligations: Provide an appropriate mechanism for the data subject to request review
Provide a process for individuals to challenge or appeal a decision and the grounds on which they can appeal
If requested, provide justification and/or reasons for the decision made
Act upon requests within specific timelines (illustrative: within 21 days of receipt; extension by 14 days where complex or multiple requests)
reviewerRequirements: Has appropriate authority and capability to change the decision
Conducts a thorough assessment of all relevant data, including any additional information the data subject provides
Active and meaningful — not 'rubber-stamping' the AI recommendation
Has authority and competence to go against the automated recommendation
Evaluates and interprets the recommendation; considers all input data and other additional factors
rights.exceptionsNote: Right to Human Review can be excluded where Exceptions apply.

sensitivePersonalData.rule: Automated decision-making involving sensitive personal data is only allowed where (a) explicit consent has been obtained from the data subject OR processing is necessary for reasons of public interest; AND (b) the data controller has put suitable measures in place to safeguard the data subject's rights.
sensitivePersonalData.explicitConsentRequirement: Consent must be clear, specific, and require a direct action from the data subject (e.g., checking a tick box, signing a document).

implementationGuidance: Maintain a log of all automated decisions affecting data subjects, with the basis for any decision (model version, input data, output, override/review history)
Build the human review pathway into the same system that produces the automated decision — not as a separate manual process
Train reviewers in the operation of the model so they can meaningfully override recommendations
Make the privacy notice describe the automated decision-making in plain language with the meaningful logic above
